title: Growth Without Price Distortion | Research Affiliates
gist: Research Affiliates' Que Nguyen argues that the two standard ways to size positions in a growth equity index—cap weighting and equal weighting—force a bad tradeoff, and proposes weighting by realized fundamental growth instead to get higher risk-adjusted returns and better style alignment. Note: the raw record is mostly site chrome and is truncated at a registration paywall, so only the Key Points and one opening paragraph carry signal.

insights.claim: The two most popular ways to size holdings in a growth equity index—market-cap weighting and equal weighting—force investors to choose between a rule that overweights expensive expectations and one that ignores magnitude.
insights.speaker: Que Nguyen
insights.so_what: Frames why standard growth-index construction may systematically misallocate capital, motivating a search for an alternative weighting scheme.
insights.quote: these approaches tend to leave investors choosing between a rule that overweights expensive expectations or one that ignores magnitude

insights.claim: In a growth equity index the position-sizing decision (how much of each stock to hold) is usually delegated to market-cap weighting, even though stock selection uses elaborate systematic rules.
insights.speaker: Que Nguyen
insights.so_what: Highlights an underexamined design choice in index funds where most investor capital actually sits.
insights.quote: The second decision—position sizing—is usually determined by market-cap weighting.
insights.locator: Every dollar in a growth equity index

insights.claim: Weighting an index by realized fundamental growth, rather than by price-cap or equal weight, is designed to deliver higher risk-adjusted returns, tighter alignment with the growth style, and more resilient performance in higher-stress periods.
insights.speaker: Que Nguyen
insights.so_what: Suggests a smart-beta growth alternative that may outperform conventional growth indices on a risk-adjusted basis, especially in drawdowns.
insights.quote: weighting based on realized fundamental growth (i.e., fundamental growth weighting), an approach designed to deliver higher risk-adjusted returns, tighter alignment with growth style, and more resilient performance in higher-stress periods

insights.claim: Investors should allocate more capital to companies that are actually growing and less to companies whose growth is already fully priced in, without sacrificing scalability.
insights.speaker: Que Nguyen
insights.so_what: The core actionable principle: tilt growth exposure toward delivered fundamentals over priced expectations while keeping the strategy capacity-friendly.
insights.quote: allocate more capital to companies that are actually growing and less to companies whose growth is more fully priced. Moreover, scalability should not be compromised.
